results.test_code:
# AUTO-GENERATED by split_acceptance_tests.py
# Each original test function was split into one function per top-level
# assertion so pytest reports per-check pass/fail counts.
import pytest
import keep.api.models.db.preset as preset_mod

def test_preset_is_private_defaults_to_false__a1():
    """
    The review discussion questioned why `is_private` exists alongside `created_by`.
    The addressed change makes `is_private` explicitly default to False (instead of being nullable/None),
    which documents the intended behavior via the model default.
    """
    Preset = preset_mod.Preset
    PresetDto = preset_mod.PresetDto
    preset_instance = Preset(tenant_id='t1', name='n1', options=[])
    assert preset_instance.is_private is False, 'Preset.is_private should default to False (not None) to clearly document non-private by default.'

def test_preset_is_private_defaults_to_false__a2():
    """
    The review discussion questioned why `is_private` exists alongside `created_by`.
    The addressed change makes `is_private` explicitly default to False (instead of being nullable/None),
    which documents the intended behavior via the model default.
    """
    Preset = preset_mod.Preset
    PresetDto = preset_mod.PresetDto
    preset_instance = Preset(tenant_id='t1', name='n1', options=[])
    dto_instance = PresetDto(id=preset_instance.id, name='n1', options=[])
    assert dto_instance.is_private is False, 'PresetDto.is_private should default to False (not None) to match the model and document default behavior.'
